title: SQL query parser fails to parse valid SELECT query with a CASE .. END in it. It fails with parser error: identifier 'x' ambiguous
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(x INTEGER, y VARCHAR(50), z DATE, w FLOAT, v BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t2(x, y, z, w, v) VALUES(1, 'test', '2023-01-01', 1.1, TRUE);
INSERT INTO t2(x, y, z, w, v) VALUES(2, 'example', '2023-02-01', 2.2, FALSE);
SELECT CASE WHEN x > 1 THEN 'greater' ELSE 'lesser' END AS comparison, y, w + 1.0, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t2 WHERE x < t2.x) FROM t2 WHERE v IS NOT NULL ORDER BY comparison, y;
SELECT x, y, CASE WHEN w < 2.0 THEN 'low' ELSE 'high' END AS level FROM t2 WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM t2 WHERE y = t2.y) ORDER BY x;
key_bug_features: SQL parser fails with CASE statement in SELECT query
